summary: Stop request logging from leaking full response bodies
problem_statement: The current request logging middleware logs full response bodies, which can contain sensitive user data, large payloads, and internal state. This creates a security and privacy risk, bloats log output, and makes logs harder to parse. Request logging should capture metadata (method, URL, status, duration) without echoing entire response payloads.
in_scope: Identify the request logging code that currently logs full response bodies
Modify the logging to capture request metadata (method, URL, status code, response time) without including response body content
Ensure the modified logging uses the logger from P6-R2-19 if available
Verify that no response body content appears in production logs
out_of_scope: Replacing the logging middleware entirely (e.g. switching to morgan) — only modify the existing approach
Adding request body logging or redaction — focus is on response body leakage
Structured logging format changes — handled separately if needed
requirements: Request logging no longer includes full response body content
Request logging still captures method, URL, status code, and response time
The logging change uses the environment-gated logger from P6-R2-19 if available
acceptance_criteria: Request logging does not leak full response bodies
Request metadata (method, URL, status, duration) is still logged
npm run check passes with no new type errors introduced
dependencies: P6-R2-19 — logger module should be available for consistent logging
risks: Developers may rely on response body logging for debugging — ensure debug-level verbose logging is still possible via the logger's environment gating
implementation_notes: If response body logging is needed for development debugging, gate it behind a debug log level so it is suppressed in production. Log only a truncated preview or byte count rather than the full body if any response content logging is preserved.
